title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Language Clinic: Safe Respawn
summary: Score drifts from destruction records, waves advance before fragments resolve, the ship respawns inside danger, pacing accelerates without respite, and assistance modes shame the player. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the safe respawn increment in fair progression and difficulty model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-score-progression-spawning-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_score_progression_spawning_3(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Respawn evidence · Theory Safe Respawn
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-safe-respawn-s82a9n" that uses case 4124 to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Respawn evidence · Theory Safe Respawn.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Safe Respawn into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Respawn evidence · Theory Safe Respawn, keep Safe Respawn connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-safe-respawn-s82a9n
# Competency: asteroids-safe-respawn.
# Case 4124: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_safe_respawn_s82a9n(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_s82a9n = "s82a9n-n9a28s-4124"
    assert evidence_variant_s82a9n
    evidence_axes_s82a9n = (
        "players82a9n",
        "seed4124",
        "inputn9a28s",
        "frames82a9n4124",
        "state4124s82a9n",
        "playtestn9a28s4124",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_s82a9n)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Safe Respawn evidence · Theory Safe Respawn, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 1149 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Safe Respawn model · Theory Safe Respawn
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Safe Respawn: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5292 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Safe Respawn model · Theory Safe Respawn.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
